subject: Sports
title: Caffeine: The Legal Performance Drug
description: Learn Caffeine: The Legal Performance Drug in one minute.

hook.text: Almost every elite athlete uses it before competition. It's legal, cheap, and backed by hundreds of studies.

buildup.text: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors in the brain, reducing perceived effort and delaying fatigue.

discovery.text: Studies show caffeine improves endurance by 2-4% and power output by 3%. In elite sport, that's the margin of victory.

twist.text: Caffeine was on the Olympic banned substance list until 2004. Now virtually every endurance athlete uses it.

climax.text: Optimal dosing is 3-6 mg per kilogram of body weight, taken 30-60 minutes before competition.

punchline.text: The world's most popular drug is also sport's most effective legal boost.

quiz.question: How does caffeine improve athletic performance?
options: It blocks adenosine receptors, reducing perceived effort
It thins the blood for better circulation
